page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute and memory operations. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations, which contribute to strong Passmark CPU extended instruction scores and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression. In memory-bound tasks, the DDR5 configuration achieves top-tier cached read-only bandwidth and strong uncached read-only bandwidth, though uncached write bandwidth remains in the average tier. For language model workloads, the server demonstrates strong prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium models, but drops to average performance during text generation on larger 70B models.

The c8i-flex.12xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile within the compute-optimized category, utilizing a flex-tier resource allocation that provides dedicated compute power without the premium of fully rigid instance types. This resource density makes it appropriate for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from high-frequency Intel Xeon cores and advanced vector instructions. Suitable workloads include software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs at 3.9 GHz, and 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the server relies on network-attached storage and provides 11 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression, static web serving, and image processing, alongside strong results in software compilation and database operations. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) accelerates machine learning tasks, delivering strong prompt processing for small and medium language models. This configuration is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring forty-eight vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores at 3.9 GHz
96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction support
11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier performance in static web serving and single-threaded compression
Appropriate for software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference
